summary: Proposal r6_pump_high_liq_only_long is mathematically healthy: single supported categorical filter, computable at T0, structurally distinct from existing multi-filter R6 archetypes, with adequate-to-ample expected sample size on the sparse R6 channel.

distinct_from_existing_ids: existing R6 long archetypes using 2+ filters (per rationale; all current R6 entries use multi-filter conjunctions)
analyses.distinctness_notes: Sparse-channel single-dimension adaptation: all existing R6 archetypes stack 2+ filters (which the registry note says crash on R6's ~800 base events). A single high-liquidity gate is structurally distinct — it represents the minimal-conjunction sparse-channel design pattern, not a verbatim duplicate of any existing slug.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: R6 base ~800 events/30d × event_type=pump implicit (~0.5 of channel) × liquidity_bucket=high (~0.33 of universe assuming roughly equal low/mid/high split, likely higher since high-liq symbols dominate volume) × long side (already specified, no further cut) = 800 × 0.5 × 0.7 ≈ 280. Conservative midpoint; could be 200–400 depending on actual high-liq concentration in R6 triggers.
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: With only 1 filter and ~280 expected triggers, this archetype has broad exit compatibility. Strongly favor `fixed` family (only positive-mean family in coverage: mean +0.175, median +0.217, 91% positive). Avoid time_only (mean -0.73) and multi_tp (mean -0.28) which dominate the negative tail. Given R2 lesson on E126 asymmetric TP/SL trap, recommend the GPU loop sweep tight TP/SL ratios (TP/SL ≥ 0.5) under fixed exits and validate with mean/kelly_capped metrics, not legacy p25.
